mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and compresses equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 45% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital expenditure recovery, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 60% of synchronized expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, rising industrial production confirms capacity utilization peaks and margin expansion, which sustains positive S&P 500 forward returns despite elevated valuation multiples.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in late-cycle regimes, or the mechanism reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 82%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no statistical relationship with GDP growth, or positive returns occur in less than 40% of above-trend GDP quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 200, persistent inflation erodes real corporate profits and forces monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of high-inflation periods, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening, which boosts wage-driven consumption and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined payroll-unemployment signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, declining retail sales signal demand destruction and margin compression, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during tightening periods, or the signal becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for interest rate changes.
